briefdescription.para: codec BCLK provider, codec FSYNC provider
codec BCLK consumer, codec FSYNC provider
codec BCLK provider, codec FSYNC consumer
codec BCLK consumer, codec FSYNC consumer

detaileddescription.para: This is used to describe all of the possible clock-related configurations w.r.t the DAI and the codec.

briefdescription.para: TDM, FSYNC asserted at the same time as MSB.

detaileddescription.para: The communication between the DAI and the CODEC may use different protocols depending on the scenario.

briefdescription.para: no BCLK inversion, no FSYNC inversion

detaileddescription.para: Some applications may require a different clock polarity (FSYNC/BCLK) compared to the default one chosen based on the protocol.
The type of the DAI. This ID type is used to configure bespoke DAI HW settings.

DAIs have a lot of physical link feature variability and therefore need different configuration data to cater for different use cases. We usually need to pass extra bespoke configuration prior to DAI start.

para.verbatim: The interface was initialized but is not yet ready to receive / transmit data. Call dai_config_set() to configure interface and change its state to READY.

briefdescription.para: The interface is ready to receive / transmit data.
The interface is receiving / transmitting data.
The interface is clocking but not receiving / transmitting data.
The interface is draining its transmit queue.
TX buffer underrun or RX buffer overrun has occurred.
Start the transmission / reception of data.

para.verbatim: If DAI_DIR_TX is set some data has to be queued for transmission by the dai_write() function. This trigger can be used in READY state only and changes the interface state to RUNNING.

briefdescription.para: Optional - Pre Start the transmission / reception of data.

para.verbatim: Allows the DAI and downstream codecs to prepare for audio Tx/Rx by starting any required clocks for downstream PLL/FLL locking.

briefdescription.para: Stop the transmission / reception of data.

para.verbatim: Stop the transmission / reception of data at the end of the current memory block. This trigger can be used in RUNNING state only and at first changes the interface state to STOPPING. When the current TX / RX block is transmitted / received the state is changed to READY. Subsequent START trigger will resume transmission / reception where it stopped.

briefdescription.para: Pause the transmission / reception of data.

para.verbatim: Pause the transmission / reception of data at the end of the current memory block. Behavior is implementation specific but usually this state doesn't completely stop the clocks or transmission. The DAI could be transmitting 0's (silence), but it is not consuming data from outside.

briefdescription.para: Optional - Post Stop the transmission / reception of data.

para.verbatim: Allows the DAI and downstream codecs to shutdown cleanly after audio Tx/Rx by stopping any required clocks for downstream audio completion.

Send all data in the transmit queue and stop the transmission. If the trigger is applied to the RX queue it has the same effect as DAI_TRIGGER_STOP. This trigger can be used in RUNNING state only and at first changes the interface state to STOPPING. When all TX blocks are transmitted the state is changed to READY.
Stop the transmission / reception immediately and discard the contents of the respective queue. This trigger can be used in any state other than NOT_READY and changes the interface state to READY.

briefdescription.para: Prepare the queues after underrun/overrun error has occurred.

para.verbatim: This trigger can be used in ERROR state only and changes the interface state to READY.
This trigger frees resources and moves the driver back to initial state.
This trigger prepares for data copying.

detaileddescription.para: The function will be called to power up the device and update for example possible reference count of the users. It can be used also to initialize internal variables and memory allocation.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
If successful.

detaileddescription.para: The function will be called to unregister/unbind the device, for example to power down the device or decrease the usage reference count.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
If successful.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, const struct dai_config *cfg, const void *bespoke_cfg, size_t size)
memberdef.name: dai_config_set

briefdescription.para: Configure operation of a DAI driver.

detaileddescription.para: The dir parameter specifies if Transmit (TX) or Receive (RX) direction will be configured by data provided via cfg parameter.
The function can be called in NOT_READY or READY state only. If executed successfully the function will change the interface state to READY.
If the function is called with the parameter cfg->frame_clk_freq set to 0 the interface state will be changed to NOT_READY.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
Pointer to the structure containing configuration parameters.
Pointer to the structure containing bespoke config.
Bespoke config size.
If successful.
Invalid argument.
DAI_DIR_BOTH value is not supported.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, struct dai_config *cfg, enum dai_dir dir)
memberdef.name: dai_config_get

briefdescription.para: Fetch configuration information of a DAI driver.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance
Pointer to the config structure to be filled by the instance
Stream direction: RX or TX as defined by DAI_DIR_*

simplesect.para: 0 if success, negative if invalid parameters or DAI un-configured

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, enum dai_dir dir, int stream_id)
memberdef.name: dai_get_properties

briefdescription.para: Fetch properties of a DAI driver.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance
Stream direction: RX or TX as defined by DAI_DIR_*
Stream id: some drivers may have stream specific properties, this id specifies the stream.

simplesect.para: Pointer to the structure containing properties, or NULL if error or no properties

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, enum dai_dir dir, int stream_id, struct dai_properties *dst)
memberdef.name: dai_get_properties_copy

briefdescription.para: Fetch properties of a DAI driver.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance
Stream direction: RX or TX as defined by DAI_DIR_*
Stream id: some drivers may have stream specific properties, this id specifies the stream.
address where to write properties to
if success
if arguments are incorrect
if there are no properties for the device
if method not implemented by the driver

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, enum dai_dir dir, enum dai_trigger_cmd cmd)
memberdef.name: dai_trigger

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
Stream direction: RX, TX, or both, as defined by DAI_DIR_*. The DAI_DIR_BOTH value may not be supported by some drivers. For those, triggering need to be done separately for the RX and TX streams.
Trigger command.
If successful.
Invalid argument.
The trigger cannot be executed in the current state or a DMA channel cannot be allocated.
RX/TX memory block not available.
DAI_DIR_BOTH value is not supported.

detaileddescription.para:
dev
Component device.
cfg
Timestamp config.
Optional method.

parameterdescription.para: If successful.

detaileddescription.para:
dev
Component device.
cfg
Timestamp config.
Optional method

parameterdescription.para: If successful.

detaileddescription.para:
dev
Component device.
cfg
Timestamp config.
Optional method.

parameterdescription.para: If successful.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, struct dai_ts_cfg *cfg, struct dai_ts_data *tsd)
memberdef.name: dai_ts_get

detaileddescription.para:
dev
Component device.
cfg
Timestamp config.
tsd
Receives timestamp data.
Optional method.

parameterdescription.para: If successful.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, const void *bespoke_cfg, size_t size)
memberdef.name: dai_config_update

detaileddescription.para: This function updates the configuration of a DAI interface at runtime. It allows setting bespoke configuration parameters that are specific to the DAI implementation, enabling updates outside of the regular flow with the full configuration blob. The details of the bespoke configuration are specific to each DAI implementation. This function should only be called when the DAI is in the READY state, ensuring that the configuration updates are applied before data transmission or reception begins.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
Pointer to the buffer containing bespoke configuration parameters.
Size of the bespoke_cfg buffer in bytes.
If successful.
If the configuration update operation is not implemented.
Negative errno code if failure.

briefdescription.para: Used to extract the clock configuration from the format attribute of struct dai_config.
Used to extract the protocol from the format attribute of struct dai_config.
Used to extract the clock inversion from the format attribute of struct dai_config.

detaileddescription.para: The DAI API provides support for the standard I2S (SSP) and its common variants. It supports also DMIC, HDA and SDW backends. The API has a config function with bespoke data argument for device/vendor specific config. There are also optional timestamping functions to get device specific audio clock time.
